metadata.name: Parametric Decoy #30
metadata.description:
The project started with the discovery of an unusual shape. Draw two parallel sine arcs and connect their opposite points. That's how you get one slice of Parametric Decoy. The shape may appear in its different phases. Sometimes elegant, sometimes awkward. Sometimes broken, sometimes symmetrical. Only connectivity stays the same.

Features:
- size: small, medium, large
- color: *several options*
- background: transparent, halftone, cutout
- distorted: no, maybe*, twisted, displaced, yes**

* "maybe" means I'm not quite sure if distortion affects visible part of the shape
** "yes" means combination of "twisted" and "displaced"

Press S to save. To download in custom size (ex. 2000px width), add &size=2000 to url, reload and press S.

Made with p5js, inspired by pop-art collages and my own geometric sketches

metadata.name: La Pedrera Serie 1 #96
metadata.description:
This project is inspired from Gaudí's Casa Milà in Barcelona, also known as La Pedrera. 

This is the first part of a 2-part-serie based on La Pedrera.

In this project, which is intentionaly saturated in color, you will recognize an abstract reproduction of the curved floors and windows of La Casa Milà.

The colors are accentued by the flowers disseminated on each floor. 

Main features of this project include:
- Palette: 10 colors,
- Texture: Strong or Soft,
- Noise: True or False,
- Strong Lines: True or False,
- Flower Packing: Mini or Maxi.

In live mode, press [s] to save a 1200 x 1500 (4 DPI) version of the mint.

Thanks a lot to everyone who will mint a token! :)

metadata.name: Celestial #262
metadata.description:
Abstract celestial art.
Best viewed in the live mode.

Live mode options:
Press "f" - full screen
Press "s" - save PNG (2700x3600)

Created with p5js.

May 2022
ykxotkx

metadata.name: Hypericum #156
metadata.description:
This system translates color into movement. A swarm of drawing particles, like virtual pencils, are guided by the color values of the pixels in an input image. The formulas performing the conversion put into play many parameters. The space of possibilities of the system is enormous, the variety of drawings endless. 256 is just a small arbitrary sample. 

This work can be seen as a playful reflection on the concept of “style”, which is so essential to our ideas about art and (human) artists. This short program can paint the same image on infinitely many “styles” – each of them reducible to a simple combination of mathematical parameters. 

The input image is a photo of a yellow hypericum flower I took during a holiday in the Argentinean Patagonia, near Bariloche, in 2016.

Better on landscape format (optimal ratio is 3:2) > on your computer, [open live]
Press [s] to download capture.

Made by Leonardo Solaas
TW > @solaas
IG > @leonardo_solaas
WWW > solaas.com.ar

metadata.name: Kintsugi #321
metadata.description:
Kintsugi is a philosophy, it's the concept of highlighting imperfections, visualizing patches and seams on the area to focus on, rather than focusing on missing details.

This art of precious scars is a technique used as a means of analyzing the idea of ​​loss through synthesis and restoration of the destroyed and a metaphor for the possibility of rebirth.
Recovery from destructive events is possible through care and patching, not through hiding the damage, but by accepting a new state of the object or the whole life.
The world is a vase that is endlessly disassembled and sewn together again, continuing to bear traces of each event.

Non-human entities helped us put these vases together. In the beginning, there were vague forms, these were the shards themselves, created in collaboration with Latent Diffusion. Over time, they have grown in detail, drawing their stories more clearly, thanks to Jax Diffusion.

The code that puts everything together was written by Yulikolay, for which he is greatly appreciated.

metadata.name: Fermi paradox #212
metadata.description:
"Two possibilities exist: Either we are alone in the Universe or we are not. Both are equally terrifying."

Sir Arthur C. Clarke
--

A search for extraterrestrial life using p5.js
• press [S] to save a 1600x2000 image (.png)

Designed for Chrome, tested on Edge
Licensed under CC BY-NC-SA

metadata.name: Flossom #36
metadata.description: This work is a research of flow fields, exploring generative art forms and colors. It uses various drawing methods, randomized color palettes, and layers of patterns to add multiple dimensions of variety to the work. Willing to create a visual blossom.

metadata.name: PRIMAL #89
metadata.description:
primal tries to channel the pelts of some extinct beasts in a patchwork style arrangement :)

this is a side project I have been working on and off for quite some time and I think it is finally ready to be released into the wild.

enjoy!

metadata.name: Rush #83
metadata.description:
I am excited to show my new project "Rush". The art is programmatically drawn using basic shapes, math, random using p5js.

To Save Image in different resolution:

press 'd' or '0' to save in default quality
press '1' to save as 1280x1706px
press 'm' or '2' to save in medium quality - 1920x2560px
press 's' or '3' to save in fine quality - 2880x3840px
press 'p' or '4' to save in printable quality - 3456x4608px
press '5' to save as 4147x5529px
press '6' to save as 4976x6635px
press 'h' or '7' to save in highest quality - 5971x7962px

To add or remove border line from frame:

press 'r' to add border line and
press 'backspace' to remove border line

press 'f' to see art in full screen.

metadata.name: Rebug #58
metadata.description:
A debug view of the underlying particulate algorithm of Atoms' Gaze and Quantum Folly. Refined as a gift for collectors.

Enjoy watching the structures form. Once it's fully rendered you can download a full resolution image of 3508 x 4961 px by ***double clicking/tapping***. At this size the piece is printable at A3 size at 300 dpi. If you're interested in acquiring a signed print please contact @KRANKARTA.

Created on Chrome desktop 103.0 on 2022-08-06 in javascript and GLSL. Results on other browsers/devices may vary.

metadata.name: Zephyr #137
metadata.description:
The name Zephyr traces back to Zephyrus, the Greek god of the west wind, and refers to any light breeze. The flowing particles get pushed around by a zephyr, creating interesting and organic patterns.

Live Mode Controls:

- Default is 2000x2000 pixel canvas. Press “1” for 1000x1000px canvas, “3” for 3000x3000px, “4” for 4000x4000px
- Press 'p' to save a png of the canvas.

Created using p5.js

Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0

metadata.name: Mado #101
metadata.description:
Windows, viewports, openings into another space. “Mado” is your view into a dimension of ease: Gentle curves caress the landscape. Thin streams of matter open wide to reveal unseen detail, each fiber following its neighbors with its own approach. Three point brackets frame curves into subcompositions and help you to focus on the texture: “What are these fibers made of?”

What can you glimpse through the window, and what is hidden?

Press ’s’ to save a .png

metadata.name: Building a Monument #89
metadata.description:
Made with P5
By Olivier Mattia
Twitter: @0xm4tt1a.

KEYS:
d: "one day, one picture" mode
s: save png
1-8: change resolution

Copyright (c) 2022 0xma.
Licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0

metadata.name: Ersbla #491
metadata.description:
Ersbla is part of the fxhash live minting experience at Proof of People, London 2022. 

It is the third entry in a series of abstract generative experiments inspired by the graphic and textile work of Anni and Josef Albers. As a follow-up to the 'Sabler' and 'Lebras' collections, 'Ersbla' produces textile effects using a combination of thousands of tiny dots arranged on variable grids, resulting in a wide variety of unique Bauhaus-inspired compositions in a variety of palettes.

Press 'S' to save a high-resolution 2400 x 3396 px image in PNG format.

Depending on the density of your output, please allow up to a few seconds for your edition to generate.

metadata.name: Breakers #319
metadata.description:
Companion project to Neural Collapse (#15780), for the collectors of my previous efforts.
A study on the repetition and stacking of a simple dynamic structure, this time inside a cylinder.

Everything procedurally generated at runtime.

Interactivity : 
- You can click on each individual breaker to open or close them. The problem is it will toggle others.
- Press and hold the mouse button to slightly move the viewpoint. The previous interaction not being disabled while doing that, you get some switching all over the place as a bonus bug/feature.

- Press "a" to increase quality of the antialiasing (3 levels), at the cost of performances. If you don't like jaggies and your machine can take it.
- Export a PNG capture at various resolutions by pressing the number keys 1 to 9 (top row or numpad, and only in live mode, after pressing "open"). Resolutions ranges from 720 to 7680 pixels. I recommend starting with "4".

GPU recommended. Best performances on Chrome in general.

metadata.name: The muon in the hole #209
metadata.description:
Special collection for #fxhashturnsone.

Halftones, dynamic composition and contrasts.

Procedural realtime javascript / webgl.
Desktop / Chrome recommended.


Controls :
- Mouse wheel/middle/ Pinch : Zoom
- After zooming, Mouse left / Drag : Pan
- Press [r] to reset the camera.
- Press [f] to toggle the frame.
- Press [c] to fully unlock the camera, with rotation on Mouse2.
- Export a PNG capture at various resolutions by pressing the number keys [1] to [9] (top row or numpad, and only in live mode, after pressing "open"). Resolutions ranges from 720 to 7680 pixels for the smallest dimension. I recommend starting with [4] and going from there. What is captured is what's on screen, so you can get creative if you so desire.

NFT License

metadata.name: Junction: Pins #35
metadata.description: An assortment of connection structures used between Bounds constructs and their Junctions

metadata.name: Contort #115
metadata.description:
Contort is the result of combining several previously meaningless experiments and learnings into a single cohesive algorithm. The project comes in a variety of shapes, bends, twists, colors, and other variations.  

The artwork builds towards the end-result by rendering a fixed number of forms per frame to ensure a smooth rendering process. Rendering times can vary depending on which features are combined by the hash.

metadata.name: Sugar & Splice #297
metadata.description:
'Sugar & Splice'

Vibrant somethings, full of flavor, dancing within conjoined boundaries.

Controls:
s: Saves an image of the canvas with the current settings
1-6: Scales the canvas size, from 1 (default, 2048px x 2792px) to 6 (12288px x  16752px)
g: Toggles grain texture
t: Toggles background transparency
f: Toggles fit to screen (layout and other factors will change based on the new size, but overall style, colors, etc will remain the same)
p: Shortcut for large print settings: disables the grain texture and scales the canvas to the largest setting
b: Toggles b-side 

teaboswell 2022
CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, See bundle.js.LICENSE.txt for third party license information
